E: Arctura pricing framework; operation economics analysis
F: Mission-critical: autonomous picking, zone transfer, mixed-case depalletizing. Justified by $2.40/min cost basis + margin. Value-based: $3.50/min on 80-min operation = $280 vs $2K-$5K manual labor equivalent.
Priority but not mission-critical: pallet sorting. High volume compensates lower per-min rate. $0.75/min × 24 ops/wk × 15 min = ~$270/wk/zone.

E: Arctura pricing framework; industry IoT monitoring benchmarks
F: Industrial IoT maintenance platforms charge $50-$150/asset/mo for predictive analytics. $40/robot/mo = entry-level, leaves margin for warehouse operator. Source: MPI Manufacturing Study 2026.
Per automated quality inspection scan requiring real-time vision processing. Covers defect detection, dimensional verification, label compliance.
Mission-critical: fully autonomous item retrieval and placement.
Core operation. First to activate at each zone.

F: Range: 12-35/wk. In-person baseline 40-200 picks/hr by site size. 10-25% autonomous conversion fraction. Conservative for large DCs, moderate for mid-size. Mostly substitution; net-new from extended shift coverage.
Lower complexity than picking. High volume offsets lower rate.
Requires sorting-specific conveyor integration after initial platform go-live.
Focused sort cycle shorter than full pick-and-place (~25-35 min).

Includes multi-stop routing, load balancing, and handoff coordination.

E: Warehouse automation adoption curves; enterprise robotics ramp benchmarks
F: Initial pilot volume. Safety certification, workflow integration, operator training.
8-quarter (2-year) ramp to maturity. Consistent with industrial robotics deployment literature.
Flagship zone: highest volume, most mature teams, earliest activation.
Slight reduction for second zone due to shared maintenance pool, scheduling friction.
Third zone: moderate reduction from robot fleet allocation constraints.
Fourth zone: shared fleet and technician bandwidth.
Fifth zone: smaller facility or later activation.
Sixth zone: smallest deployment. Blended avg (6 zones) = 83%.

F: 480 total robots ÷ 6 zones. Atlas 12 DCs with 1,200+ robots total. 80/zone = moderate density for mixed autonomous fleet.
Assumes partial fleet coverage at launch, ramping to full over 5 quarters.
Full predictive coverage within 15 months of go-live.

F: Autonomous share = subset of total QC volume. Defect detection + dimensional verification + label compliance. 250/mo conservative per both reports.
